name: Cleo Laine
abstract: Dame Cleo Laine, Lady Dankworth (born Clementine Dinah Bullock; 28 October 1927 – 24 July 2025) was a British singer and actress known for her scat singing. She was the wife of jazz composer and musician Sir John Dankworth and the mother of bassist Alec and singer Jacqui Dankworth.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I would love to have had Cleo Laine and John Dankworth, who are great friends of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jane Ira Bloom
about_quotes.evidence: Working with Cleo Laine — some kind of special feeling I had about playing with her
about_quotes.doc_title: Jane Ira Bloom interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 3, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Lorraine Feather
about_quotes.evidence: Did you send Cleo Laine your Ellington re-dos? Because she’s doing an Ellington album with Mercer Ellington. Did you send them to her?” And I was able to go, “Yes. She got back to me, she’s recording a couple of them.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Lorraine Feather interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 22, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I brought her a record by Cleo Laine, singing – what was his name? – Charles Ives. Cleo Laine singing Charles Ives... Years later, she saw Cleo on television.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: Dave’s distinctive songs have been recorded by Rosemary Clooney, Cleo Laine, Mel Torme and on his own solo recordings.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Cleo Laine; Interviewee does not respond with personal connection or knowledge of her.
